metadata.title:
URGES POSTER


BOYCOTT

CHIEF SULLIVAN BELIEVES
CHAMBER OF COMMERCE


COULD PUT A STOP TO BAR


BERS GRATING VISITORS.

pages.raw_text:
That the Chamber of Commerce
and 10000 club and all others of
the city's boosters responsible for
bringing visitors here from other
parts of the United States should
boycott grating barber shops as a
safeguard to the city's reputation,
was advocated by Chief of Police
Sullivan today.


There seems to be absolutely
no help for it other than to warn
visitors and every one else to trade
with the shops that are being run
on the square and to shun all shops
regarding which numerous COM
plants have been coming to me"
the chief said.


II would further suggest that the
public absolutely refuse to pay the
exorbitant prices being inflicted on
them. Make the BARRIERS come to
me with their complaints and their
attempts to collect the money in
such cases and l believe this thing
can soon be regulated. They will
not dare to lay their hands on a
person refusing to pay their prices.
If they do, we will take them in
hand ourselves"


Complaints came to the chief of
police last night from Edward
Grantham and c. F. Hendricks,
Wallace. Idaho, plumbers, regard
ing fragrant overcharges IN the
Grand barber shop at Howard and
Main. Grantham got about every
thing there was on the bill OF fare,
though he asked only for a shave
and hair cut and was charged
$3.50. Hendricks was in the chair
little more than half an hour and
was plucked of $5.75 for services
rendered.


Several complaints have been
made regarding similar treatment
In the Traders barber shop.
